structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Mode Primary Airflow Setpoint Mapping
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: When the Zone State Is Cooling
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In cooling mode, the cooling-loop output is mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Map cooling-loop output to active primary airflow setpoint between minimum and cooling maximum endpoints. If AHU SAT > room temperature, limit setpoint to minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. 1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.

evidence_quote: a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. 1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
evidence_text: a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. 1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband Mode Primary Airflow Setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: When the Zone State Is Deadband
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In deadband mode, the primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set primary airflow setpoint to minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. The primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.

evidence_quote: a. The primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
evidence_text: a. The primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Mode Primary Airflow Setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In heating mode, the active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set active primary airflow setpoint to minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.

evidence_quote: a. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
evidence_text: a. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Mode Coil Modulation
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The heating coil shall be modulated to maintain the discharge temperature at setpoint. Directly controlling heat off zone temperature control loop is not acceptable.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Modulate heating coil to maintain discharge temperature at setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: c. The heating coil shall be modulated to maintain the discharge temperature at setpoint. (Directly controlling heat off zone temperature control loop is not acceptable).

evidence_quote: c. The heating coil shall be modulated to maintain the discharge temperature at setpoint. (Directly controlling heat off zone temperature control loop is not acceptable).
evidence_text: c. The heating coil shall be modulated to maintain the discharge temperature at setpoint. (Directly controlling heat off zone temperature control loop is not acceptable).

structured_payload_candidate.title: VAV Damper Modulation
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured primary airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Always active when primary airflow setpoint is non-zero
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Modulate VAV damper to maintain measured primary airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.7.5.4. The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured primary airflow at setpoint.

evidence_quote: 5.7.5.4. The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured primary airflow at setpoint.
evidence_text: 5.7.5.4. The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured primary airflow at setpoint.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Run During Heating
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan shall run whenever Zone State is heating.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. Fan shall run whenever Zone State is heating.

evidence_quote: a. Fan shall run whenever Zone State is heating.
evidence_text: a. Fan shall run whenever Zone State is heating.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Run During Deadband and Cooling (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when the primary air volume is less than Voz for 1 minute and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Voz by 10% for 3 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Run fan in Deadband and Cooling when primary air volume < Voz for 1 minute; shut off when primary air volume > Voz by 10% for 3 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when the primary air volume is less than Voz for 1 minute and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Voz by 10% for 3 minutes.

evidence_quote: b. If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when the primary air volume is less than Voz for 1 minute and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Voz by 10% for 3 minutes.
evidence_text: b. If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when the primary air volume is less than Voz for 1 minute and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Voz by 10% for 3 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm Level 4
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm Suppression for Importance-Multiplier 0
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Zone Importance-Multiplier = 0 for static pressure reset T&R loop
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: c. If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Fan OFF and airflow sensor reading > max(10% of Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.7.6.4. Airflow Sensor Calibration. If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Leaking Valve Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If the valve position is 0% for 15 minutes, DAT is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), and the fan serving the zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Valve position 0% for 15 minutes, DAT > AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), and fan proven ON
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.7.6.6. Leaking Valve. If the valve position is 0% for 15 minutes, DAT is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), and the fan serving the zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Endpoints as Function of Zone Group Mode
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Endpoints as a Function of Zone Group Mode
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Active endpoints used in control logic vary depending on the mode of the Zone Group. Table 5.7.4 defines endpoints for Occupied, Cooldown, Setup, Warmup, Setback, and Unoccupied modes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Table 5.7.4 Endpoints as a Function of Zone Group Mode
Endpoint  Occupied  Cooldown  Setup  Warmup  Setback  Unoccupied
Cooling maximum  Vcool-max Vcool-max Vcool-max 0 0 0
Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0

structured_payload_candidate.title: Designer Must Ensure Ventilation Compliance
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The designer must ensure that the sum of the indirect ventilation provided by the fan plus the ventilation provided by the primary air at minimum setpoint meet Standard 62.1 requirements.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The designer must ensure that the sum of the indirect ventilation provided by the fan plus the ventilation provided by the primary air at minimum setpoint meet Standard 62.1 requirements.

evidence_quote: The designer must ensure that the sum of the indirect ventilation provided by the fan plus the ventilation provided by the primary air at minimum setpoint meet Standard 62.1 requirements.
evidence_text: The designer must ensure that the sum of the indirect ventilation provided by the fan plus the ventilation provided by the primary air at minimum setpoint meet Standard 62.1 requirements.

structured_payload_candidate.title: MaxΔT Limit per ASHRAE 90.1
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1-2016 limits overhead supply air to 11°C (20°F) above space temperature (e.g., 32°C [90°F] at 21°C [70°F] space temperature setpoint) to minimize stratification.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1-2016 limits overhead supply air to 11°C (20°F) above space temperature (e.g., 32°C [90°F] at 21°C [70°F] space temperature setpoint) to minimize stratification.

evidence_quote: ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1-2016 limits overhead supply air to 11°C (20°F) above space temperature (e.g., 32°C [90°F] at 21°C [70°F] space temperature setpoint) to minimize stratification.
evidence_text: ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1-2016 limits overhead supply air to 11°C (20°F) above space temperature (e.g., 32°C [90°F] at 21°C [70°F] space temperature setpoint) to minimize stratification.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow Sensor Calibration Threshold Determination
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow. The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow. The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.

evidence_quote: The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow. The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
evidence_text: The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow. The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
